feature: Create README.md with project overview, goals, and quick start
workflow_rationale: This is a documentation-only task for a greenfield project. The README.md already exists and appears comprehensive. This plan focuses on verification that the existing README meets all spec requirements. Simple workflow is appropriate as there's minimal work needed and no multi-service coordination required.

phases.name: Verify README Completeness
phases.description: Validate that the existing README.md meets all requirements from the spec: project overview, purpose/goals, and quick start guide for developers

subtasks.description: Verify README.md contains project overview section

verification.instructions: Review README.md lines 5-7 (Overview section) and confirm it introduces the inventory-apk project, explains it's an Android application for inventory management, and provides context for the project purpose

subtasks.expected_outcome: README.md has a clear '📋 Overview' section that introduces the project
subtasks.notes: Verified README.md contains complete project overview section at lines 5-7. Overview introduces Inventory APK as a mobile inventory management solution for Android, explains it helps businesses/individuals track and manage inventory efficiently, and provides context about managing stock levels, tracking movements, and generating reports.

subtasks.description: Verify README.md contains project goals and purpose

verification.instructions: Review README.md lines 9-36 (Purpose and Key Features sections) and confirm they explain what the project aims to achieve, why it exists, and what problems it solves

subtasks.expected_outcome: README.md has '🎯 Purpose' and '✨ Key Features' sections explaining project goals
subtasks.notes:
✅ Verification passed. README.md lines 9-36 contain comprehensive project goals and purpose:
- Purpose section (lines 9-16) explains why project exists and what problems it solves
- Key Features section (lines 18-36) outlines what project aims to achieve
- Clearly identifies: mobile-first solution solving desktop dependency, offline-first operation, real-time tracking, simplified workflow
All verification criteria met - no changes needed.

subtasks.description: Verify README.md contains developer quick start guide

verification.instructions: Review README.md lines 39-92 (Getting Started section) and confirm it provides prerequisites, installation steps, and a quick start guide for developers cloning the repo

subtasks.expected_outcome: README.md has comprehensive '🚀 Getting Started' section with prerequisites, installation, and quick start
subtasks.notes:
✅ Verification passed. README.md lines 39-92 contain comprehensive developer quick start guide:
- Prerequisites section (lines 41-48): Android Studio, JDK 11+, Android SDK API 24+, Git
- Installation section (lines 50-76): 5 detailed steps from cloning repo to building/running the app
- Quick Start Guide (lines 78-92): Step-by-step first-use instructions for developers
All verification criteria met - provides prerequisites, installation steps, and quick start guide for developers cloning the repo.

subtasks.description: Verify README.md markdown formatting is valid

verification.command: if command -v markdownlint >/dev/null 2>&1; then markdownlint README.md || echo 'Linting completed with warnings'; else echo 'markdownlint not installed - manual review recommended'; fi

subtasks.notes: Manual markdown validation completed. All markdown syntax verified correct: proper header hierarchy, valid lists, correctly formatted links, properly fenced code blocks, valid blockquotes. No critical formatting errors found. markdownlint tool not available, but thorough manual review confirms README.md follows proper markdown conventions.

phases.name: Document Findings
phases.description: Document any observations about the README and note referenced files that don't exist yet

subtasks.description: Catalog referenced documentation files that need future creation

verification.instructions: Review README.md and note all referenced files that don't exist yet: ROADMAP.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/features/. These should be tracked for future documentation tasks.

subtasks.expected_outcome: List of future documentation tasks identified
subtasks.notes:
✅ Catalog created successfully. Created FUTURE_DOCUMENTATION.md with comprehensive tracking of all missing documentation files:
- ROADMAP.md (HIGH priority, referenced 4 times)
- CONTRIBUTING.md (HIGH priority, referenced 3 times)  
- docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (MEDIUM priority, referenced 2 times)
- docs/features/ (MEDIUM priority, referenced 1 time)

All files verified as missing. Catalog includes priority levels, content requirements, recommended creation order, and verification checklist. These missing files are acceptable for greenfield project and tracked for future work.

summary.current_state: README.md already exists and appears complete
summary.startup_command: No environment setup required for documentation verification
summary.notes: This task is essentially complete - the README.md exists and appears comprehensive. The implementation plan focuses on verification that it meets all spec requirements.

acceptance_criteria: README.md explains project purpose and goals
README.md provides quick start guide for developers

verification_steps.name: Manual Review - Overview Section
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Overview section exists and is complete

verification_steps.name: Manual Review - Purpose Section
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Purpose section exists and explains project goals

verification_steps.name: Manual Review - Getting Started Section
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Getting Started section exists with prerequisites and installation steps

verification_strategy.reasoning: Trivial risk level - this is a documentation verification task with no code changes. The README already exists and appears complete. Manual review is sufficient to confirm it meets requirements.

minor_issues.title: Local settings file committed
minor_issues.description: Files with .local suffix typically should not be committed, but impact is low (only contains generic tool permissions)
